name: ls-toolbox

metadata.name: LS-toolbox
metadata.summary: Toolboxes for working with LS-Dyna and LS-Prepost through python.

documentation:
# LS_toolbox
A collection of tools for working with LS-DYNA and LS-PrePost.

## Installation
`pip install LS_toolbox`

Create environment variables named `LSDYNA_PATH` and `LSPREPOST_PATH` and set them to the paths of the LS-DYNA and LS-PrePost executables, respectively.\
To do this, open the terminal and type:

For Windows:
```bash
setx LSDYNA_PATH "path\to\lsdyna\executable"
setx LSPREPOST_PATH "path\to\lsprepost\executable"
```
For Linux:
```bash
export LSDYNA_PATH="path/to/lsdyna/executable"
export LSPREPOST_PATH="path/to/lsprepost/executable"
```

## Example usage 
### Copy/paste and run as it is:
```python
import LS_toolbox as lst
import pyvista as pv
import os
import subprocess
import numpy as np


"""
Example using a beam 3D mesh .k file.
The mesh is first displayed using PyVista.
Then a finite element model is created by setting the boundary conditions (traction test) and material properties.
Finally, the simulation is run using LS-Dyna and the d3plot file is opened in LS-PrePost.
Simulation files are cleared at the end (except for the .K file of the FE model).
"""

# get LS_toolbox path
lst_path = os.path.dirname(lst.__file__)

# Path to the example keyfile
file_path = os.path.join(lst_path, "Example/Beam_3D_mesh_example.k")

# Read the keyfile
keyfile_lines = lst.read_keyfile.read_keyfile(file_path)

# Visualize the mesh with PyVista
mesh = lst.display.keyword2pvmesh(file_path)
mesh.plot(show_edges=True)

# Get the nodes from the keyfile
nodes = lst.read_keyfile.read_nodes(file_path)
ids = np.array(list(nodes.keys()))
coords = np.array(list(nodes.values()))

# Add a fixed displacement on the bottom nodes
# Get the nodes on the bottom face
bottom_nodes = ids[coords[:, 2] == min(coords[:, 2])]
lst.write_keyfile.add_spc(keyfile_lines, bottom_nodes)

# Add a prescribed motion on the top nodes
# Get the nodes on the top face
top_nodes = ids[coords[:, 2] == max(coords[:, 2])]
lst.write_keyfile.add_prescribed_motion_velocity(keyfile_lines, top_nodes, 1.)  # Only along the z axis

# Add a section
section_id = lst.write_keyfile.add_section_solid(keyfile_lines)

# Add a material elastic linear
material_data = np.array([1000, 1e6, 0.4999])  # rho, E, nu
material_id = lst.write_keyfile.add_mat_elastic(keyfile_lines, material_data)

# Modify the part to set the section and material data
part_id = 1
lst.write_keyfile.modify_part(keyfile_lines, part_id, section_id, material_id)

# Add an implicit solver
lst.write_keyfile.add_implicit_solver(keyfile_lines, dt0=0.5)

# Add termination time
lst.write_keyfile.add_termination(keyfile_lines, 30.)

# Add d3plot output
lst.write_keyfile.add_d3plot(keyfile_lines, dt=0.5)

# Write the new keyfile
new_file_path = file_path.replace(".k", "_model.k")
lst.write_keyfile.write_keyfile(keyfile_lines, new_file_path)

# Run LS-Dyna
print("Running LS-Dyna...")
lst.prepost_commands.run_lsdyna(new_file_path)
print("Done.")

# Open the d3plot file in PrePost
subprocess.check_call(f"\"{lst.prepost_commands.LS_PREPOST_PATH}\" {os.path.join(os.path.split(new_file_path)[0], 'd3plot')}", shell=True)

# Clear simulation files
lst.clear_sim_files.clear_sim_files(os.path.split(new_file_path)[0])
```

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_mesh.read_nodes
documentation:
Read a mesh file and return the nodes and elements.
\:param mesh_file_path: Path to the mesh file.
\:return: Nodes and elements.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_mesh.read_elements
documentation:
Read a mesh file and return the elements and associated node ids.
\:param mesh_file_path: Path to the mesh file.
\:param keyword: Keyword to search for in the mesh file.
\:return: Elements table (elements and node ids) \[[elem_id, part_id, node_id1, node_id2, ...]\].

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_mesh.create_mesh
documentation:
Create a mesh from the nodes and elements tables.
\:param node_table: Nodes table.
\:param elem_table: Elements table.
\:return: Mesh.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.clear_sim_files.clear_sim_files
documentation:
Clear simulation files in the given directory.
\:param sim_dir_path: Path to the simulation directory.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.display.keyword2pvmesh
documentation:
Convert a .k file to a pyvista mesh.
\:param file_path: Path to the .k file.
\:return: Pyvista mesh (UnstructuredGrid).

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_keyfile.read_nodes
documentation:
Read a .k file and return the nodes coordinates.
\:param file_path: Path to the .k file.
\:return: Dictionary of nodes coordinates {node_id: [x, y, z]}.
Read nodes coordinates from a .k file.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_keyfile.read_keyfile
documentation:
Read a .k file and return a list of lines.
\:param file_path: Path to the .k file.
\:return: List of lines in the key file.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_keyfile.read_keyfile_dict
documentation:
Read a .k file and return a dictionary of keywords and their lines.
\:param file_path: Path to the .k file.
\:return: Dictionary of keywords and their lines {keyword: \[[lines]\]}.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_keyfile.get_ids
documentation:
Get the ids of the given key in the .k file.
\:param key: Key.
\:param list_lines: List of lines in the .k file.
\:return: List of ids.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_keyfile.read_elements
documentation:
Read a .k file and return the elements.
\:param file_path: Path to the .k file.
\:return: List of elements \[[element_id, node_id1, node_id2, ...]\].

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_stl.read_stl
documentation:
Read an stl file and return the mesh.
\:param file_path: Path to the stl file.
\:return: Mesh.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_stl.read_obj
documentation:
Read an obj file and return the vertices.
\:param file_path: Path to the obj file.
\:return: Vertices.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_stl.get_id_from_coords
documentation:
Get the id of the closest node to the given coordinates.
\:param coords: Coordinates.
\:param nodes: Nodes.
\:return: Node id.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_stl.read_json_landmarks
documentation:
Read a json file containing landmarks and return the landmarks.
\:param landmark_file_path: Path to the json file.
\:return: Landmarks.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_stl.read_sml
documentation:
Read an sml file and return the data.
\:param sml_file_path: Path to the sml file.
\:return: data.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.read_stl.modify_sml_by_landmark_index
documentation:
Modify the landmark's index in the sml data.
\:param sml_data: Sml data.
\:param landmark_name: Landmark name.
\:param index: Index.
\:return: Modified sml data.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.prepost_commands.run_prepost
documentation:
Run LS-PrePost with the given commands file.
\:param commands_path: Path to the commands file.
\:param ls_prepost_path: Path to the LS-PrePost executable.
\:param clean_generated_files: If True, the ls prepost generated files will be deleted after the execution.
\:param verbose: If True, the LS-PrePost output will be displayed in the console.
\:return: None

functions.name: LS_toolbox.prepost_commands.extract_nodalcoords_from_file
documentation:
Extract nodal value (strain, stress, etc.) from text file.
\:param file_path: Path to the file containing the nodes' ids and value.
\:param time: if not None, returns only the value at the specified time (nearest time available in file).
\:return: Dictionary {time: {node_id: value}}

functions.name: LS_toolbox.prepost_commands.extract_nodalvalue_from_file
documentation:
Extract nodal value (strain, stress, etc.) from text file.
\:param file_path: Path to the file containing the nodes' ids and value.
\:param time: if not None, returns only the value at the specified time (nearest time available in file).
\:return: Dictionary {time: {node_id: value}}

functions.name: LS_toolbox.prepost_commands.get_nodal_results
documentation:
Get the nodal results of a part in a d3plot file.
\:param d3plot_path: Path to the d3plot file.
\:param part_id: Part id.
\:param fringe_nb: Fringe number corresponding to the desired results.
\:return: Dictionary {time: {node_id: value}}

functions.name: LS_toolbox.prepost_commands.create_node_set
documentation:
Create a node set.
\:param command_file: Path to the command file.
\:param node_ids: List of node ids.
\:param set_id: Set id.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.prepost_commands.run_lsdyna
documentation:
Run LS-Dyna.
\:param dyna_file: Path to the LS-Dyna file.
\:param ncpu: Number of CPUs to use.
\:param verbose: If True, the LS-Dyna output will be displayed in the console.
\:return: None

variables.name: LS_toolbox.prepost_commands.NCPU
documentation: Number of CPUs to use for LS-Dyna

variables.name: LS_toolbox.prepost_commands.VERBOSE
documentation: If True, the LS-PrePost and LS-Dyna output will be displayed in the console

variables.name: LS_toolbox.prepost_commands.LS_PREPOST_PATH
documentation: Path to the LS-PrePost executable

variables.name: LS_toolbox.prepost_commands.LS_DYNA_PATH
documentation: Path to the LS-Dyna executable

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.write_keyfile
documentation:
Write the key file.
\:param list_lines: List of lines in the key file.
\:param file_path: Path to the .k file.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.write_keyfile_dict
documentation:
Write a dictionary of keywords and their lines to a .k file.
\:param file_path: Path to the .k file.
\:param keyfile_dict: Dictionary of keywords and their lines {keyword: \[[lines]\]}.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_node_set
documentation:
Add node set to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param node_ids: List of node ids that will be in the node set.
\:return: Node set id.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_spc
documentation:
Add spc boundary condition to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param node_ids: List of node ids that will be fixed.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_curve
documentation:
Add curve to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param curve_data: Curve data \[[x, y]\].
\:return: Curve id.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_vector
documentation:
Add vector to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param vector_data: Vector data [x, y, z].
\:return: Vector id.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_prescribed_motion_velocity
documentation:
Add prescribed motion velocity to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param node_ids: List of node ids that will be prescribed motion.
\:param velocity: Prescribed motion velocity.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_section_solid
documentation:
Add section solid to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:return: Section id.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_mat_elastic
documentation:
Add material elastic to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param material_data: Material data [rho, E, nu].
\:return: Material id.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_mat_ogden
documentation:
Add material Ogden (MAT_077) to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param material_data: Material data [rho, nu, mu1, alpha1].
\:return: Material id.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.modify_part
documentation:
Modify part in a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param part_id: Part id.
\:param section_id: Section id.
\:param material_id: Material id.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.modify_elementsolid2ortho
documentation:
Modify element solid to solid_ortho in a .k file.
\:param a: Vector of first principal direction.
\:param b: Vector of second principal direction.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_mat_ortho
documentation:
Add material orthotropic to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param material_data: Material data [rho, Ea, Eb, Ec, nuba, nuca, nucb, Gab, Gbc, Gca].
\:return: Material id.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_mat_hgo
documentation:
Add material Holzapfel-Gasser-Ogden (MAT_295) to a .k file.
ONLY WORKS WITH EXPLICIT SOLVER.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param material_data: Material data [rho, nu, K1, K2].
\:return: Material id.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_mat_soft_tissue
documentation:
Add material soft tissue to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param material_data: Material data [rho, C1, C2, C3, C4, C5, Bulk modulus, Stretch ratio].
\:return: Material id.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_erosion
documentation:
Add erosion to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param material_id: Material id to which the erosion will be added.
\:param max_princ_stress: Maximum principal stress for erosion.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_implicit_solver
documentation:
Add implicit solver to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param dt0: Initial time step.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_control_timestep
documentation:
Add control timestep to a .k file, specifically a mass scaling.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param dt2ms: Minimum time step. If timestep is under this value, mass scaling will be applied to the concerned elements.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_d3plot
documentation:
Add d3plot bin out to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param dt: Time step for d3plot results.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_termination
documentation:
Add termination to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param end_time: End time of the simulation.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.modify_node_coordinates
documentation:
Modify node coordinates in a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
\:param nodes_dict: Dictionary of nodes coordinates {node_id: [x, y, z]}.

functions.name: LS_toolbox.write_keyfile.add_hourglass_energy
documentation:
Add hourglass energy computation to a .k file.
\:param list_lines: lines in the key file (see read_keyfile).
